query_used: Google NotebookLM features Deep Research learning capabilities
findings: ResearchFinding(title='Direct Link 1', url='https://notebooklm.google/', snippet='Content provided directly by user.', full_content='Google NotebookLM | AI Research Tool & Thinking Partner', relevance_score=1.0)

ResearchFinding(title='Direct Link 2', url='https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/models-and-research/google-labs/notebooklm-deep-research-file-types/', snippet='Content provided directly by user.', full_content='NotebookLM adds Deep Research and support for more source types
A strong set of trusted sources is the foundation of any research project, and the foundation of how you use NotebookLM. Today, NotebookLM is introducing new ways to help you find and use sources more effectively: using Deep Research agents and supporting more of the file types you use every day.
Expand your sources with Deep Research
We are now rolling out Deep Research to automate and simplify complex online research. It acts like your dedicated researcher, synthesising a detailed report or recommending relevant articles, papers or websites — and you can direct this "researcher" to search specific places, too.
With your guidance, Deep Research takes your question, creates a research plan and browses hundreds of websites on your behalf, refining its search as it learns. In a few minutes, it generates an organized, insightful, source-grounded report.
Here’s what makes NotebookLM's Deep Research different: the report is just the beginning. You can add the report and its sources directly into your notebook. You can continue to add other sources while Deep Research runs in the background, helping you assemble a rich knowledge base on any topic without leaving your workflow. And you can then use any of NotebookLM’s capabilities, such as Audio or Video overviews, to pull insights or transform content to understand the topic better.
Here’s how to try Deep Research in NotebookLM:
- Start your search: In the source panel, select Web as a source.
- Choose your research style:
- Fast Research: Best for a quick search. It rapidly scans for information, letting you immediately review and import sources.
- Deep Research: Best for a full briefing. It performs an in-depth analysis to find high-quality sources. This runs in the background so you can continue your work.
Upload information in more file types
Your work spans multiple file types, and your research tool should, too. Based on your feedback, we’re adding support for new file types:
- Google Sheets: Add structured data and ask information on key statistics or generate summaries from your spreadsheets.
- Drive files as URLs: Add drive files as URLs, with a simple copy-paste, just like any website or YouTube video. You can even add multiple links, separated with commas
- Images: Upload a photo of your hand-written notes or a brochure you got.
- PDFs from Google Drive: Add research papers, reports or ebooks directly from Drive, skipping downloading and re-uploading.
- Microsoft Word documents (.docx): Upload your .docx files to analyze notes, drafts and other documents.
All these features will be available to all users over the next week with images becoming available over the next few weeks. We’re looking forward to seeing how you’ll boost your research with all these new sources in NotebookLM.', relevance_score=0.9)

ResearchFinding(title='Direct Link 3', url='https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/models-and-research/google-labs/notebooklm-student-features/', snippet='Content provided directly by user.', full_content="6 ways to use NotebookLM to master any subject
Turning information into knowledge is challenging, whether you are preparing for an exam, starting a new project or exploring a new interest. That’s why we built NotebookLM as your personalized AI partner for research and thinking. It helps you make connections and understand complex material faster.
This summer, we introduced learning features like Video Overviews, which students worldwide have used for everything from understanding a topic quickly to preparing for exams.
Today, we are introducing new features and public notebooks that transform passive reading into active learning. Here’s what’s new.
1. Recall information with flashcards and quizzes.
Actively engaging with material is key to learning. NotebookLM now instantly creates study aids from your documents, including lecture notes, research papers or work reports. These interactive tools are grounded entirely in your sources.
You can customize the topic, set the difficulty and share your study sets with friends or colleagues with a simple link. Both flashcards and quizzes give you the option to dig deeper; just click “explain” and NotebookLM will generate a detailed overview, helping you understand a flashcard definition or explaining why you got a quiz question wrong, with citations pointing back to the original source material.
2. Create tailored, high-quality documents with upgraded reports.
One of NotebookLM’s most popular features is the ability to Instantly generate Briefing Docs and Study Guides from sources. Users have asked us for more reports, and for more control over them.
We are introducing redesigned reports, which include a new Blog Post format. These reports also dynamically suggest options based on the theme, topic or industry mentioned in your sources. For example, upload a scholarly article on economic theory and NotebookLM might suggest a glossary of key terms or a magazine-style explainer of the paper’s implications; upload a draft of a short story and NotebookLM might suggest a character analysis or a detailed critique of the plot.
You can also create new custom formats to generate content tailored to your specific needs.
3. Deepen conversations with Learning Guide.
We're introducing a new “Learning Guide” option that encourages participation with probing, open-ended questions. Instead of just giving answers, it helps you break down problems step-by-step and adapts explanations to your needs, building a deeper understanding of the subject. Learning Guide is like having a personal tutor for your sources directly within NotebookLM. Chat customization and Learning Guide will start being available to all users starting next week.
4. Learn with trusted academic content.
We have partnered with OpenStax, the leading provider of free, peer-reviewed textbooks, to transform their most popular academic titles into interactive Notebooks. These ready-to-use Notebooks help high school and college students learn with high-quality, vetted sources. We have curated notebooks on these subjects:
5. Hear your sources in a new way with Audio Overviews.
Audio Overviews help you understand your material on the go. Based on the popularity of Deep Dives, we've added new formats to provide more diverse perspectives on your resources. For example, use the Debate format to explore two sides of a topic before a class discussion. The new formats include:
- Brief: A single AI host delivers a short summary of your sources' core ideas.
- Critique: Two AI hosts review your material, offering constructive feedback on an essay or design document.
- Debate: Two AI hosts discuss different perspectives on the topics in your sources.
6. Give teachers and students expanded access.
Today, educators can create Notebooks from their class materials and assign them directly in Canvas by Instructure and PowerSchool Schoology Learning using Gemini LTI. In the coming weeks, educators will also be able to create and assign notebooks in Google Classroom. This expands on the access we announced for teenagers and users on education domains earlier this summer.
Get started today
These new features make NotebookLM a more powerful partner for deepening your understanding of the world.
Ready to get started? Flashcards, Quizzes and Reports will start to be available today and will expand to more users over the next week. The new Audio Overview formats are available to everyone today. You can also explore our new OpenStax Notebooks and let us know what you think.", relevance_score=0.8)
key_facts: NotebookLM is Google's AI research tool and thinking partner
Deep Research feature acts as a dedicated researcher that synthesizes detailed reports
Deep Research can browse hundreds of websites on behalf of users
The system creates a research plan and refines its search as it learns
Generated reports are organized, insightful, and source-grounded
Users can add reports and their sources directly into their notebook
Deep Research runs in the background while users continue adding other sources
Video Overviews allow users to understand topics quickly
NotebookLM creates flashcards and quizzes from documents like lecture notes and research papers
Study aids are entirely grounded in the user's sources
Users can customize topic and difficulty for study materials
Study sets can be shared with others via simple links
The 'Explain' feature provides detailed overviews with citations pointing to original sources
NotebookLM transforms passive reading into active learning
The tool supports more file types than when it was first released
structured_summary: Google NotebookLM is an AI-powered research tool designed as a thinking partner for research and learning. It has evolved to include Deep Research capabilities that act as a dedicated researcher, synthesizing detailed reports by browsing hundreds of websites and creating organized, source-grounded reports based on user queries. Users can add these reports and their sources directly into their notebooks while Deep Research runs in the background. For learning purposes, NotebookLM offers Video Overviews, flashcards, and quizzes generated from user documents such as lecture notes and research papers. These study aids are customizable by topic and difficulty, shareable via links, and include an 'Explain' feature that provides detailed overviews with citations to original sources. The platform transforms passive reading into active learning and supports an expanded range of file types.
